heading: Federal Law Enforcement Training Centers

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall maintain in the Department the Federal Law Enforcement Training Centers (FLETC), headed by a Director, who shall report to the Secretary.

children.heading: Position
children.label: b Position
children.text: The Director shall occupy a career-reserved position within the Senior Executive Service.
develop training goals and establish strategic and tactical organizational program plan and priorities;

provide direction and management for FLETC’s training facilities, programs, and support activities while ensuring that organizational program goals and priorities are executed in an effective and efficient manner;

develop homeland security and law enforcement training curricula, including curricula related to domestic preparedness and response to threats or acts of terrorism, for Federal, State, local, tribal, territorial, and international law enforcement and security agencies and private sector security agencies;

monitor progress toward strategic and tactical FLETC plans regarding training curricula, including curricula related to domestic preparedness and response to threats or acts of terrorism, and facilities;

ensure the timely dissemination of homeland security information as necessary to Federal, State, local, tribal, territorial, and international law enforcement and security agencies and the private sector to achieve the training goals for such entities, in accordance with paragraph (1);

children.label: i
children.text: Federal law;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Federal Acquisition Regulation, including requirements regarding agency obligations to contract only with responsible prospective contractors; and

children.label: iii
children.text: Department acquisition management directives; and

children.label: B
children.text: maximizes opportunities for small business participation;
coordinate and share information with the heads of relevant components and offices on digital learning and training resources, as appropriate;

advise the Secretary on matters relating to executive level policy and program administration of Federal, State, local, tribal, territorial, and international law enforcement and security training activities and private sector security agency training activities, including training activities related to domestic preparedness and response to threats or acts of terrorism;

collaborate with the Secretary and relevant officials at other Federal departments and agencies, as appropriate, to improve international instructional development, training, and technical assistance provided by the Federal Government to foreign law enforcement; and
carry out such other functions as the Secretary determines are appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: evaluate best practices of law enforcement training methods and curriculum content to maintain state-of-the-art expertise in adult learning methodology;

children.label: B
children.text: provide expertise and technical assistance, including on domestic preparedness and response to threats or acts of terrorism, to Federal, State, local, tribal, territorial, and international law enforcement and security agencies and private sector security agencies; and

children.label: C
children.text: maintain a performance evaluation process for students.

children.heading: Relationship with law enforcement agencies
children.label: 2 Relationship with law enforcement agencies
children.text: The Director shall consult with relevant law enforcement and security agencies in the development and delivery of FLETC’s training programs.

children.heading: Training delivery locations
children.label: 3 Training delivery locations
children.text: The training required under paragraph (1) may be conducted at FLETC facilities, at appropriate off-site locations, or by distributed learning.

children.label: i
children.text: execute strategic partnerships with State and local law enforcement to provide such law enforcement with specific training, including maritime law enforcement training; and

children.label: ii
children.text: coordinate with the Director of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency and with private sector stakeholders, including critical infrastructure owners and operators, to provide training pertinent to improving coordination, security, and resiliency of critical infrastructure.

children.heading: Provision of information
children.label: B Provision of information
children.text: The Director shall provide to the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate, upon request, information on activities undertaken in the previous year pursuant to subparagraph (A).

children.heading: FLETC details to DHS
children.label: 5 FLETC details to DHS
children.text: The Director may detail employees of FLETC to positions throughout the Department in furtherance of improving the effectiveness and quality of training provided by the Department and, as appropriate, the development of critical departmental programs and initiatives.

children.heading: Detail of instructors to FLETC
children.label: 6 Detail of instructors to FLETC
children.text: Partner organizations that wish to participate in FLETC training programs shall assign non-reimbursable detailed instructors to FLETC for designated time periods to support all training programs at FLETC, as appropriate. The Director shall determine the number of detailed instructors that is proportional to the number of training hours requested by each partner organization scheduled by FLETC for each fiscal year. If a partner organization is unable to provide a proportional number of detailed instructors, such partner organization shall reimburse FLETC for the salary equivalent for such detailed instructors, as appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: Salaries, travel expenses, lodging expenses, and miscellaneous per diem allowances of their personnel attending training courses at FLETC.

children.label: ii
children.text: Salaries and travel expenses of instructors and support personnel involved in conducting advanced training at FLETC for partner organization personnel and the cost of expendable supplies and special equipment for such training, unless such supplies and equipment are common to FLETC-conducted training and have been included in FLETC’s budget for the applicable fiscal year.

children.heading: Excess basic and advanced Federal training
children.label: B Excess basic and advanced Federal training
children.text: All hours of advanced training and hours of basic training provided in excess of the training for which appropriations were made available shall be paid by the partner organizations and provided to FLETC on a reimbursable basis in accordance with .section 4104 of title 5

children.label: i
children.text: State, local, tribal, and territorial law enforcement personnel.

children.label: ii
children.text: Foreign law enforcement officials, including provision of such training at the International Law Enforcement Academies wherever established.

children.label: iii
children.text: Private sector security officers, participants in the Federal Flight Deck Officer program under , and other appropriate private sector individuals.section 44921 of title 49

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: B Waiver
children.text: The Director may waive the requirement for reimbursement of any cost under this section and shall maintain records regarding the reasons for any requirements so waived.

children.heading: Reimbursement
children.label: 9 Reimbursement
children.text: The Director is authorized to reimburse travel or other expenses for non-Federal personnel who attend activities related to training sponsored by FLETC, at travel and per diem rates established by the General Services Administration.

children.label: A
children.text: Athletic and related activities.

children.label: B
children.text: Short-term medical services.

children.label: C
children.text: Chaplain services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Director is authorized to appoint and maintain, as necessary, Federal annuitants who have expert knowledge and experience to meet the training responsibilities under this subsection.

children.heading: No reduction in retirement pay
children.label: B No reduction in retirement pay
children.text: A Federal annuitant employed pursuant to this paragraph shall not be subject to any reduction in pay for annuity allocable to the period of actual employment under the provisions of section 8344 or 8468 of title 5 or similar provision of any other retirement system for employees.

children.heading: Re-employed annuitants
children.label: C Re-employed annuitants
children.text: A Federal annuitant employed pursuant to this paragraph shall not be considered an employee for purposes of subchapter III of chapter 83 or chapter 84 of title 5 or such other retirement system (referred to in subparagraph (B)) as may apply.

children.heading: Counting
children.label: D Counting
children.text: Federal annuitants shall be counted on a full time equivalent basis.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: E Limitation
children.text: No appointment under this paragraph may be made which would result in the displacement of any employee.

children.heading: Travel for intermittent employees
children.label: 12 Travel for intermittent employees
children.text: The Director is authorized to reimburse intermittent Federal employees traveling from outside a commuting distance (to be predetermined by the Director) for travel expenses.

children.heading: On-FLETC housing
children.label: e On-FLETC housing
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, individuals attending training at any FLETC facility shall, to the extent practicable and in accordance with FLETC policy, reside in on-FLETC or FLETC-provided housing.
expend funds for public awareness and to enhance community support of law enforcement training, including the advertisement of available law enforcement training programs;

accept and use gifts of property, both real and personal, and to accept gifts of services, for purposes that promote the functions of the Director pursuant to subsection (c) and the training responsibilities of the Director under subsection (d);

accept reimbursement from other Federal agencies for the construction or renovation of training and support facilities and the use of equipment and technology on government owned-property;1So in original. Probably should be “Government-owned property;”.1

obligate funds in anticipation of reimbursements from agencies receiving training at FLETC, except that total obligations at the end of a fiscal year may not exceed total budgetary resources available at the end of such fiscal year;

children.label: A
children.text: purchase employee and student uniforms; and

children.label: B
children.text: purchase and lease passenger motor vehicles, including vehicles for police-type use;
provide room and board for student interns; and
expend funds each fiscal year to honor and memorialize FLETC graduates who have died in the line of duty.

children.heading: Basic training
children.label: 1 Basic training
children.text: The term “basic training” means the entry-level training required to instill in new Federal law enforcement personnel fundamental knowledge of criminal laws, law enforcement and investigative techniques, laws and rules of evidence, rules of criminal procedure, constitutional rights, search and seizure, and related issues.

children.heading: Detailed instructors
children.label: 2 Detailed instructors
children.text: The term “detailed instructors” means personnel who are assigned to the Federal Law Enforcement Training Centers for a period of time to serve as instructors for the purpose of conducting basic and advanced training.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 3 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Federal Law Enforcement Training Centers.

children.heading: Distributed learning
children.label: 4 Distributed learning
children.text: The term “distributed learning” means education in which students take academic courses by accessing information and communicating with the instructor, from various locations, on an individual basis, over a computer network or via other technologies.

children.heading: Employee
children.label: 5 Employee
children.text: The term “employee” has the meaning given such term in .section 2105 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: an Executive Department as defined in ;section 101 of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: an independent establishment as defined in ;section 104 of title 5

children.label: C
children.text: a Government corporation as defined in ;section 9101 of title 31

children.label: D
children.text: the Government Printing Office;

children.label: E
children.text: the United States Capitol Police;

children.label: F
children.text: the United States Supreme Court Police; and

children.label: G
children.text: Government agencies with law enforcement related duties.

children.heading: Law enforcement personnel
children.label: 7 Law enforcement personnel
children.text: The term “law enforcement personnel” means an individual, including criminal investigators (commonly known as “agents”) and uniformed police (commonly known as “officers”), who has statutory authority to search, seize, make arrests, or to carry firearms.

children.label: A
children.text: of or pertaining to any county, parish, municipality, city, town, township, rural community, unincorporated town or village, local public authority, educational institution, special district, intrastate district, council of governments (regardless of whether the council of governments is incorporated as a nonprofit corporation under State law), regional or interstate government entity, any agency or instrumentality of a local government, or any other political subdivision of a State; and

children.label: B
children.text: an Indian tribe or authorized tribal organization, or in Alaska a Native village or Alaska Regional Native Corporation.

children.heading: Partner organization
children.label: 9 Partner organization
children.text: The term “partner organization” means any Federal agency participating in FLETC’s training programs under a formal memorandum of understanding.

children.heading: State
children.label: 10 State
children.text: The term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and any possession of the United States.

children.heading: Student intern
children.label: 11 Student intern
children.text: The term “student intern” means any eligible baccalaureate or graduate degree student participating in FLETC’s College Intern Program.

children.heading: Prohibition on new funding
children.label: h Prohibition on new funding
children.text: No funds are authorized to carry out this section. This section shall be carried out using amounts otherwise appropriated or made available for such purpose.
